title: Do I understand this
question: Do you understand what the tag-filter change actually did?
lede: The five-slice tag-filter build is merged locally. Before you rely on it, check your model of it against the code that actually landed.

model.lede: Where a catalog render comes from now, versus before the change.

before.label: Static list, rendered once at authoring time

after.label: URL drives two pure functions; the DOM is re-derived

behaviorsTitle: Three behaviors a diff skim won't tell you
behaviorsLede: Each one is deliberate — the reasoning came out of the build session, not the diff.

behaviors.title: The no-JS page is complete before any script runs
behaviors.what: index.html ships the full catalog with every tag chip as a plain ?tag= link; app.mjs only re-renders after load.
behaviors.why: The spec's no-JS criterion is not a fallback — it is the baseline the script enhances. Chips work as normal navigation without JavaScript.

behaviors.title: Unknown tags render the accepted empty state, not a blank list
behaviors.what: filterByTag returns [] for unknown tags; the glue renders emptyState(tag) whenever the visible list is empty and a tag is present.
behaviors.why: STOP-01 resolved that unknown tags stay empty — no remote suggestions — and interface-directions chose the explain-and-suggest empty state.

behaviors.title: Back and forward re-run the same render path
behaviors.what: popstate calls the identical renderFromUrl used at load; there is no separate history handler.
behaviors.why: One render path means back/forward can never drift from a fresh load of the same URL.

options.label: Only in the browser, after load
In Node tests and the browser — same import
Only in Node, at materialize time

questions.explanation: It is a plain export from src/catalog.mjs; node --test imports it directly and app.mjs imports the same file in the browser.

questions.prompt: With JavaScript disabled, what does ?tag=git show?

options.label: The full catalog — chips are plain links, filtering needs JS
Only git-tagged skills — the server filters

questions.explanation: There is no server; the static page always carries the complete catalog, and filtering is a client-side enhancement.

questions.prompt: What renders for ?tag=deploy (a tag no skill has)?
questions.explanation: STOP-01 kept unknown tags empty and interface-directions chose direction B: the empty state names the miss and offers the real tag set.

questions.prompt: What state does the page persist between visits?

options.label: The last-used filter, kept in browser storage
Nothing — the URL is the only state
A session cookie with the tag

questions.explanation: Persistence was an explicit non-goal at Gate A; the single ?tag= parameter carries all state, which is why back/forward just works.

questions.prompt: Why does popstate reuse renderFromUrl instead of its own handler?

options.label: So back/forward can never diverge from a fresh load

questions.explanation: One render path is the whole invariant: any URL renders identically whether you arrived by load, click, or history traversal.

questions.prompt: What was rejected at STOP-01 during the build?

options.label: Fetching tag suggestions from a remote API

questions.explanation: Mid-session scope addition contradicted the approved non-goal (no network calls); it was rejected and the plan continued as specified.
